# Model3 { #paleobeasts.Model3 }

```python
Model3(
    forcing,
    var_name='ice volume',
    f1=-16,
    f2=16,
    t1=30,
    t2=10,
    vc=1.4,
    state_variables=['v', 'k'],
    non_integrated_state_vars=['k'],
    diagnostic_variables=['insolation'],
    *args,
    **kwargs,
)
```

Model 3 from Ganopolski (2024) describing glacial cycle evolution under orbital forcing.

The model tracks ice volume ``v`` and glacial regime ``k`` (1 = glaciation,
2 = deglaciation).  The ice volume relaxes toward a forcing-dependent
equilibrium:

    dv/dt = (ve(f) - v) / t1    when k=1  (glaciation)
    dv/dt = -vc / t2             when k=2  (deglaciation)

Regime switches follow:

- k=1 → k=2  if v > vc, df/dt > 0, and f > 0
- k=2 → k=1  if f < f1

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                 | Description                                                                                      | Default        |
|----------|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| forcing  | pb.core.Forcing                      | Orbital insolation forcing ``f(t)`` (W m\ :sup:`-2`).  Required.                                 | _required_     |
| var_name | str                                  | Label for the model output.  Default ``'ice volume'``.                                           | `'ice volume'` |
| f1       | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Insolation threshold for glacial inception (W m\ :sup:`-2`; typically -20 to -15).  Default -16. | `-16`          |
| f2       | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Insolation threshold for deglaciation inception (W m\ :sup:`-2`; positive).  Default 16.         | `16`           |
| t1       | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Relaxation timescale for glacial inception (kyr).  Default 30.                                   | `30`           |
| t2       | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Relaxation timescale for deglaciation (kyr).  Default 10.                                        | `10`           |
| vc       | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Critical ice volume controlling dominant periodicity and asymmetry. Default 1.4.                 | `1.4`          |

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

State variables are ``v`` (ice volume, normalised) and ``k`` (regime
index, non-integrated).  The diagnostic variable ``insolation`` records
``f(t)`` at each output step.

The internal derivative of forcing ``dfdt`` is computed via
:func:`calc_df` by default; supply a custom callable or
``pb.core.Forcing`` to override.

Parameter defaults are taken from Ganopolski (2024).  Time-varying
parameters follow the standard callable contract ``(t)``,
``(t, state)``, or ``(t, state, model)``.

## References {.doc-section .doc-section-references}

Ganopolski, A. (2024). Glacial cycles. *Nature Reviews Earth &
Environment*, 5, 89–106.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

.. code-block:: python

    import paleobeasts as pb
    from paleobeasts.signal_models.g24 import Model3, calc_f

    orbital_forcing = pb.core.Forcing(calc_f)
    model = Model3(forcing=orbital_forcing)
    output = model.integrate(
        t_span=(-2000, 0), y0=[0.0, 1], method='RK45',
        kwargs={'max_step': 0.5}
    )
    ts = output.to_pyleo(var_names=['v'])

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [calc_dfdt](#paleobeasts.Model3.calc_dfdt) | Evaluate df/dt — the time derivative of the orbital forcing at time ``t``. |
| [calc_vc](#paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vc) | Evaluate the critical ice volume ``vc`` (constant or time/state-varying). |
| [calc_ve](#paleobeasts.Model3.calc_ve) | Equilibrium ice volume toward which the system is attracted. |
| [calc_vg](#paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vg) | Glacial equilibrium ice volume. |
| [calc_vu](#paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vu) | Unstable equilibrium ice volume separating glacial and interglacial basins. |
| [dydt](#paleobeasts.Model3.dydt) | Evaluate ice-volume tendency at time ``t`` and state ``x``. |

### calc_dfdt { #paleobeasts.Model3.calc_dfdt }

```python
Model3.calc_dfdt(t, x=None)
```

Evaluate df/dt — the time derivative of the orbital forcing at time ``t``.

### calc_vc { #paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vc }

```python
Model3.calc_vc(t, x=None)
```

Evaluate the critical ice volume ``vc`` (constant or time/state-varying).

### calc_ve { #paleobeasts.Model3.calc_ve }

```python
Model3.calc_ve(v, f, f1, f2, vi=0)
```

Equilibrium ice volume toward which the system is attracted.

In the bi-stable regime (``f1 < f < f2``) the target depends on the
current ice volume ``v`` relative to the unstable equilibrium ``vu``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                      | Default    |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------------|------------|
| v      | float  | Current ice volume (normalised).                 | _required_ |
| f      | float  | Orbital forcing value (W m\ :sup:`-2`).          | _required_ |
| f1     | float  | Lower insolation threshold (glaciation onset).   | _required_ |
| f2     | float  | Upper insolation threshold (deglaciation onset). | _required_ |
| vi     | float  | Interglacial equilibrium ice volume.  Default 0. | `0`        |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                    |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------|
| ve     | float  | Target equilibrium ice volume. |

### calc_vg { #paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vg }

```python
Model3.calc_vg(f, f1, f2)
```

Glacial equilibrium ice volume.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                             | Default    |
|--------|--------|-----------------------------------------|------------|
| f      | float  | Orbital forcing value (W m\ :sup:`-2`). | _required_ |
| f1     | float  | Lower insolation threshold.             | _required_ |
| f2     | float  | Upper insolation threshold.             | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                     |
|--------|--------|---------------------------------|
| vg     | float  | Glacial equilibrium ice volume. |

### calc_vu { #paleobeasts.Model3.calc_vu }

```python
Model3.calc_vu(f, f1, f2)
```

Unstable equilibrium ice volume separating glacial and interglacial basins.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                             | Default    |
|--------|--------|-----------------------------------------|------------|
| f      | float  | Orbital forcing value (W m\ :sup:`-2`). | _required_ |
| f1     | float  | Lower insolation threshold.             | _required_ |
| f2     | float  | Upper insolation threshold.             | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                      |
|--------|--------|----------------------------------|
| vu     | float  | Unstable equilibrium ice volume. |

### dydt { #paleobeasts.Model3.dydt }

```python
Model3.dydt(t, x)
```

Evaluate ice-volume tendency at time ``t`` and state ``x``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type          | Description                                                                                                        |
|--------|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | list of float | ``[dvdt]`` — the ice-volume tendency.  The regime index ``k`` is a non-integrated state variable updated in-place. |
